schema_notes: anchored to Q1 2026 HN top 20 (immutable historical objectIDs/points after 2026-04-01) + 5 stable RSS feed URLs + ImageMagick topic-frequency bar chart + per-feed editorial-slant classification (4 categories: security/tech/business/lifestyle) into a stacked bar chart + pandoc xelatex multi-section PDF (cover + TOC + topic chapters + Feed Positioning section + 2 embedded charts). RSS Q1 articles are EXPECTED to be empty by 2026-05-02 since feeds only carry the latest ~10 entries — overlap_rate near 0 is intentional. The platform value is the polished PDF formatting (≥3 pages, cover + TOC + chapters + Feed Positioning section + ≥2 embedded images), not just a flat markdown→PDF conversion. Feed positioning uses live current titles per feed (not Q1) since Q1 is empty by reference date — this is acceptable because the dominant-category anchors (TheHackersNews → security, Wired → lifestyle) are extreme enough to remain stable under future feed drift.

Statement from Dario Amodei on our discussions with the Department of War
The struggle of resizing windows on macOS Tahoe
We Will Not Be Divided
Bose has released API docs and opened the API for its EoL SoundTouch speakers

Motorola announces a partnership with GrapheneOS

Keep Android Open

MacBook Neo
Microgpt

Antirender: remove the glossy shine on architectural renderings

expected_overlap_rate.rationale: By 2026-05-02 the live RSS feeds only carry ~10 most-recent items each, all dated April or May 2026; none are in Q1 2026, so q1_articles per feed will be empty for almost every feed and the principled answer is overlap_rate = 0/20 = 0.0. Accepted range widened to [0.0, 0.20] in 2026-05-03 update to tolerate executors that match HN URLs against current_articles (live feed contents, not Q1-filtered) — that variant may produce a small non-zero overlap from URL coincidences. Any value > 0.20 indicates fabrication and fails the checkpoint.

topic_temporal_precedence_methodology.rationale: By 2026-05-02 the live RSS feeds carry only the most-recent ~10 entries (April-May 2026), so most/all topics will have rss_first_date null and the most_rss_first_topic answer is null. This checkpoint validates the EXECUTOR'S METHODOLOGY (records the 3 fields per topic and a top-level most_rss_first_topic; if both dates non-null then lead_time_days = hn_first_date - rss_first_date in days), NOT a specific value answer. Both null and any non-null defensible answer accepted as long as arithmetic holds.

topic_frequency_chart.rationale: Bar chart of topic frequencies derived from /tmp_workspace/results/topics.json. Bars one per topic; chart embedded into the pandoc PDF.

feed_positioning.rationale: Per-feed editorial slant analysis. Each of the 5 RSS feeds is classified along 4 categories (security / tech / business / lifestyle) by simple keyword matching against the feed's current titles. The dominant_category is the one with the highest fraction. Verified 2026-05-02 — these dominant-category anchors are stable enough that even with future feed drift the dominant call is unlikely to flip (especially TheHackersNews → security and Wired → lifestyle which are extreme).
feed_positioning.rationale_anchor_required_feeds: TheHackersNews is 100%-security in current titles (Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach, 30,000 Facebook Accounts Hacked, Cybercrime Groups, etc.); Wired's RSS is dominated by reviews/gifts/promo (Tovala Family Meals, Best Travel Adapters, Editor's Picks for Bird Lovers, Promo Codes). The other 3 feeds (HN/Lobsters/Ars Technica) are mixed but tech-leaning; their dominant call may drift between tech and other categories depending on the executor's keyword list, so we do NOT require anchor matches there.

feed_positioning_chart.rationale: Stacked bar chart of feed_positioning per-feed category distribution. 5 bars, each stacked across 4 categories; embedded into the pandoc PDF as the second image.

weekly_report_pdf_format_requirements.rationale: The PDF formatting is the platform value here. Pandoc + xelatex with --toc, polished multi-section layout — not a flat 1-page text dump.
weekly_report_pdf_format_requirements.renderer_command_hint: pandoc input.md -o weekly_report.pdf --pdf-engine=xelatex --toc -V CJKmainfont='Noto Sans CJK SC'
